questions.q: Which demon-king ruled the city of Lanka in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Lanka was the capital and stronghold of the ten-headed rakshasa emperor Ravana.

questions.q: Lanka is most famously described as a city gleaming with what precious material?
questions.explanation: Lanka is celebrated in the epic as a golden city radiant with wealth.

questions.q: Lanka was surrounded on all sides by what natural barrier?
questions.explanation: Lanka was an island-fortress ringed by the ocean, separating it from the mainland.

questions.q: Who were the demon-folk that inhabited Lanka?
questions.explanation: Lanka was the home of the rakshasas, the demon race over whom Ravana ruled.

questions.q: Which captive princess was held in Lanka after being abducted?
questions.explanation: Ravana carried Sita to Lanka and held her captive, the act that triggered the war.

questions.q: Which devoted vanara first leapt across the ocean to reach Lanka in search of Sita?
questions.explanation: Hanuman made the great leap over the sea to Lanka to find Sita.

questions.q: In which grove within Lanka was Sita kept captive?
questions.explanation: Sita was confined in the Ashoka Vatika, a grove of ashoka trees in Lanka.

questions.q: Whose army crossed the sea to lay siege to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama led an army of vanaras and bears across the sea to besiege Lanka.

questions.q: What did Hanuman set on fire during his visit to Lanka?
questions.explanation: With his burning tail, Hanuman set the golden city of Lanka ablaze before leaping home.

questions.q: Who became the new king of Lanka after Ravana was slain?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who joined Rama, was crowned king of Lanka.

questions.q: According to tradition, who was the original builder or possessor of Lanka before Ravana seized it?
questions.explanation: Lanka was held by Kubera, the god of wealth, before his half-brother Ravana drove him out and took it.

questions.q: Which divine architect is traditionally credited with constructing the golden city of Lanka?
questions.explanation: The celestial craftsman Vishvakarma is said to have built the magnificent golden Lanka.

questions.q: Lanka was situated at the southern peak of which mountain in the epic's geography?
questions.explanation: The city of Lanka stood upon the heights of Mount Trikuta, the three-peaked mountain.

questions.q: Who was the guardian deity or spirit of Lanka whom Hanuman defeated to enter the city?
questions.explanation: Lankini, the female guardian spirit of the city's gates, was struck by Hanuman as he entered Lanka.

questions.q: Which kindly rakshasi in Lanka comforted Sita with a reassuring dream of Rama's victory?
questions.explanation: Trijata, a gentle demoness among Sita's guards, told of a dream foretelling Ravana's fall.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's chief queen residing in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Mandodari was the wise and virtuous chief queen of Ravana in Lanka.

questions.q: What token did Hanuman carry to Sita in Lanka to prove he came from Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman showed Sita Rama's signet ring as proof of his mission.

questions.q: Which crest-jewel did Sita entrust to Hanuman in Lanka to carry back to Rama?
questions.explanation: Sita gave Hanuman her Chudamani, a hair-jewel, as a sign she still lived.

questions.q: By what means did Rama's army finally reach the island of Lanka across the sea?
questions.explanation: The vanaras built a causeway of stones, the Setu, across the ocean to Lanka.

questions.q: Which colossal sleeping brother of Ravana was roused to defend Lanka in battle?
questions.explanation: Kumbhakarna, cursed to sleep for long periods, was awakened to fight for Lanka.

questions.q: Which son of Ravana, a master of illusion, was a chief defender of Lanka and slain by Lakshmana?
questions.explanation: Indrajit (Meghnad), Ravana's sorcerer-son, defended Lanka until Lakshmana killed him.

questions.q: Which of Ravana's brothers urged returning Sita and was cast out of Lanka for it?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana counselled returning Sita, was exiled from Lanka, and joined Rama.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, how far across the sea was Lanka said to lie from the mainland shore?
questions.explanation: Lanka was described as lying a hundred yojanas across the ocean, the span of Hanuman's leap.

questions.q: Whom did Ravana defeat to take Lanka and the Pushpaka Vimana for himself?
questions.explanation: Ravana wrested Lanka and the flying Pushpaka chariot from his half-brother Kubera.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's veteran commander-in-chief and chief minister who fell defending Lanka?
questions.explanation: Prahasta, Ravana's seasoned general and minister, was slain by the vanaras before Lanka's walls.

questions.q: Through which gate of Lanka, in tradition, did the host commanded directly by Rama's allies make their main assault?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the principal assault came against the northern gate, where Ravana himself often stood.

questions.q: What was the original name said to have been given to the island of Lanka when it was first the abode of Kubera?
questions.explanation: The fortress city stood on Mount Trikuta, the three-peaked mount on which golden Lanka was raised.

questions.q: Which sea-demoness, dwelling in the waters on the way to Lanka, seized creatures by their shadows and was slain by Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Simhika, the shadow-catching sea-demon, was destroyed by Hanuman during his crossing to Lanka.

questions.q: Which divine weapon did Rama finally use to slay Ravana and end the war over Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama loosed the irresistible Brahmastra to slay Ravana and free the world of his tyranny.

questions.q: After the fall of Lanka, on which aerial chariot did Rama and Sita return to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama, Sita, and their companions flew home to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka Vimana taken from Ravana.
